varieties.name: Queensland Arrowroot 'Standard'
varieties.origin: South America (Andean region), cultivated in Queensland since 1870s
varieties.traits: 2-3m tall, large banana-like green leaves, red flowers, clump-forming
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, 5-10kg per plant when mature
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (NSW), Green Harvest (QLD), Food Forest Seeds Australia, Edible Jungle Nursery (QLD)

varieties.name: Queensland Arrowroot 'Rojo'
varieties.origin: Selected cultivar, South American origin
varieties.traits: Red-tinged foliage, 2-2.5m tall, ornamental red flowers
varieties.availability: Edible Jungle Nursery (humid subtropical climates/QLD), specialty permaculture nurseries

varieties.name: Canna indica 'Purple Arrowroot'
varieties.origin: Central/South America, heirloom cultivar, 4000+ years cultivation history
varieties.traits: 1.5-2.5m tall, violet-green to purple-tinted foliage, flame-red flowers
varieties.yield_notes: 22-50 tons per hectare (commercial), 3-8kg per plant (home garden)
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, ornamental canna suppliers (edible cultivar)

varieties.name: West Indian Arrowroot 'Standard' (Maranta arundinacea)
varieties.origin: Amazon rainforest, domesticated 8200 BC, traditional Caribbean cultivar
varieties.traits: 0.3-1.5m tall, lanceolate green leaves, small white flowers, clump-forming
varieties.availability: Tradewinds Fruit (international), specialty tropical plant suppliers

varieties.name: Maranta arundinacea 'Variegata'
varieties.origin: Selected cultivar from wild type, ornamental selection
varieties.traits: 30-90cm tall, white and green variegated foliage, small white flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than standard cultivar, primarily ornamental
varieties.availability: Ornamental plant nurseries, specialty foliage suppliers

varieties.name: Canna x generalis 'Tropicanna'
varieties.origin: Hybrid cultivar, modern breeding
varieties.traits: 1.2-1.8m tall, striped leaves (green/red/yellow), orange flowers, ornamental

varieties.name: Canna 'Black Knight'
varieties.origin: Hybrid cultivar, ornamental breeding
varieties.traits: 1.5-2m tall, dark purple-bronze foliage, deep red flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, ornamental priority over food production
varieties.availability: Garden centers, humid subtropical climates nurseries, landscape suppliers

varieties.name: East Indian Arrowroot (Curcuma angustifolia)
varieties.origin: Indian subcontinent, traditional cultivar, Himalayan foothills
varieties.traits: 1-1.5m tall, narrow leaves, yellow flowers with pink bracts, ginger family
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, Asian herb nurseries, online Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Polynesian Arrowroot (Tacca leontopetaloides)
varieties.origin: Tropical Africa/Southeast Asia, Polynesian introduction to Pacific
varieties.traits: Herbaceous perennial to 1m, large divided leaves (30-70cm), unusual bat-like flowers
varieties.availability: Rare, specialty tropical plant collectors, botanical gardens may have specimens

varieties.name: Vellakoova / Vella Koova (White Arrowroot) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional tropical monsoon climates cultivar, introduced centuries ago, now widely naturalized
varieties.traits: 60-180cm tall, green lanceolate leaves, white flowers, clump-forming
varieties.yield_notes: Good, first-year harvest December-January, 10-12 month maturity
varieties.availability: Widely available tropical monsoon climates - backyard cultivation, local farmers, agricultural markets in Kasaragod, Malappuram, Kozhikode, Wayanad

varieties.name: Manjakoova / Yellow Arrowroot 🏆
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Western Ghats and tropical monsoon climates, traditional medicinal plant
varieties.traits: 1-1.5m tall, narrow leaves, yellow flowers with pink bracts, aromatic rhizomes
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, second-year harvest (left in soil longer than white variety)
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates agricultural areas - Malappuram, Kozhikode, Wayanad, Kannur, Kasaragod; local herb suppliers

varieties.name: Neelakoova / Blue Arrowroot 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional tropical monsoon climates cultivar, possibly indigenous
varieties.traits: Similar to yellow arrowroot, slightly different leaf structure
varieties.availability: Limited - traditional farmers in tropical monsoon climates, specialty herb collectors

varieties.name: Canna edulis 'Standard Type'
varieties.origin: South American, introduced to India, cultivated in tropical regions
varieties.traits: 2-3m tall, large green leaves, red flowers
varieties.yield_notes: High, 6-8 months to harvest possible in tropical climate
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural suppliers, can be sourced from tropical plant growers

varieties.name: Canna indica 'Purple Arrowroot'
varieties.origin: Traditional tropical cultivar, 4000+ years history
varieties.traits: 1.5-2.5m tall, purple-tinted foliage, red flowers
varieties.yield_notes: High in tropical conditions, 22-50 tons/hectare commercially
varieties.availability: Available from Indian nurseries, tropical plant suppliers

varieties.name: Achira 'Nativa' Type
varieties.origin: Colombia, traditional cultivar, adaptable to various tropical regions
varieties.traits: 2-3m tall, standard green foliage, red-orange flowers
varieties.availability: Rare in India, would require international sourcing

varieties.name: Achira 'Negra' Type
varieties.origin: Colombian ecotype, dark-foliaged selection
varieties.traits: 2-3m tall, dark green to bronze foliage
varieties.availability: Rare in India, specialty collectors only

varieties.name: Achira 'Verde' Type
varieties.origin: Colombian ecotype, green variety
varieties.traits: Standard green foliage
varieties.availability: Rare in India

varieties.name: Achira 'Morada' Type
varieties.origin: Colombian ecotype, purple variety
varieties.traits: Purple-tinted foliage
varieties.availability: Rare in India

varieties.name: Tacca leontopetaloides (Polynesian Arrowroot) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to tropical Asia/Africa, naturalized in Southeast Asia
varieties.traits: Herbaceous perennial to 1m, large deeply-divided leaves, unusual flowers
varieties.availability: Rare in India, may be found in botanical collections, specialty tropical plant sources

varieties.name: St. Vincent 'Creole' Type
varieties.origin: St. Vincent, Caribbean, traditional smallholder cultivar
varieties.traits: 60-180cm tall, white rhizomes
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than 'Banana' type but more drought tolerant
varieties.availability: Rare outside Caribbean, would require international sourcing

varieties.name: St. Vincent 'Banana' Type
varieties.origin: St. Vincent, Caribbean, estate production cultivar
varieties.traits: Compact, white rhizomes
varieties.availability: Rare outside Caribbean, international sourcing required

varieties.name: Dominica Reddish Type
varieties.origin: Dominica, Caribbean islands
varieties.traits: Similar to standard type
varieties.availability: Very rare, Caribbean germplasm only
